18	    """A dataclass mixin for configuring model training parameters.
19	
20	    Args:
21	        per_device_train_batch_size (int): The batch size per GPU/TPU core for training. Defaults to 1.
22	        per_device_eval_batch_size (int): The batch size per GPU/TPU core for evaluation. Defaults to 1.
23	        gradient_accumulation_steps (Optional[int]): The number of update steps to accumulate gradients for before
24	            performing an optimizer step.
25	        tuner_backend (Optional[str]): The backend to use for parameter-efficient fine-tuning (e.g., 'peft'). Defaults
26	            to None.
27	        gradient_checkpointing (bool): If True, use gradient checkpointing to save memory at the cost of a slower
28	            backward pass. Defaults to True.
29	        vit_gradient_checkpointing (Optional[bool]): A specific gradient checkpointing setting for the Vision
30	            Transformer part of the model. Defaults to None.
31	        gradient_checkpointing_kwargs (Optional[Union[dict, str]]): Keyword arguments for
32	            `torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint`. Defaults to None.
33	        logging_first_step (bool): Whether to log the first global step. Defaults to True.
34	        logging_steps (int): Log every `logging_steps` global steps. Defaults to 5.
35	        router_aux_loss_coef (float): The coefficient for the router auxiliary loss in Mixture-of-Experts models.
36	            Defaults to 0.0.
37	        enable_dft_loss (bool): Whether to enable Diversity-from-Diversity (DFD) loss.
38	            See https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.05629. Defaults to False.
39	        enable_channel_loss (bool): Whether to enable channel loss. Defaults to False.
40	        weight_decay (float): The weight decay to apply (if not zero) to all layers except bias and LayerNorm weights.
41	            Defaults to 0.1.
42	        adam_beta2 (float): The beta2 hyperparameter for the AdamW optimizer. Defaults to 0.95.
43	        lr_scheduler_type (str): The learning rate scheduler type to use. Defaults to 'cosine'.
44	        lr_scheduler_kwargs (Optional[Union[dict, str]]): Additional keyword arguments for the learning rate scheduler,
45	            passed as a JSON string or a dictionary. Defaults to None.
46	        report_to (List[str]): The list of integrations to report results to (e.g., 'tensorboard', 'wandb'). Defaults
47	            to ['tensorboard']. If you specify `--report_to wandb`, you can set the project name through `WANDB_PROJECT`
48	            and specify the API KEY corresponding to your account through `WANDB_API_KEY`.
49	        dataloader_num_workers (Optional[int]): The number of subprocesses to use for data loading. Defaults to None.
50	        dataloader_persistent_workers (bool): If True, the data loader workers will not be shut down after a dataset
51	            has been consumed once. Defaults to False.
52	        dataloader_prefetch_factor (Optional[int]): The number of batches loaded in advance by each worker. Defaults
53	            to None.
54	        use_liger_kernel (bool): Whether to use the Liger kernel for optimization. Defaults to False.
55	        check_model (bool): If True, checks local model files for corruption or modification and provides a warning.
56	            Should be set to False in an offline environment. Defaults to True.
57	        acc_strategy (Literal['token', 'seq']): The strategy for calculating accuracy during training and validation.
58	            Can be 'token' for token-level accuracy or 'seq' for sequence-level accuracy. Defaults to 'token'.
59	        train_dataloader_shuffle (bool): Whether to shuffle the training data. Defaults to True.
60	        group_by_length (bool): Whether to group samples with approximately the same length together in the
61	            training dataset (with a random factor).
62	        max_epochs (Optional[int]): The total number of training epochs to perform. Overrides `num_train_epochs`.
63	            Defaults to None.
64	        aligner_lr (Optional[float]): A specific learning rate for the aligner part of the model. Defaults to None.
65	        vit_lr (Optional[float]): A specific learning rate for the Vision Transformer part of the model. Defaults to
66	            None.
67	        use_logits_to_keep (Optional[bool]): If enabled, reduces VRAM usage and speeds up training by calculating and
68	            storing only the necessary logits based on the labels during the forward pass. If None, the behavior is
69	            automatically determined. Defaults to None.
70	        ds3_gather_for_generation (bool): In DeepSpeed ZeRO-3, whether to gather model parameters for generation.
71	            Defaults to True.
72	        resume_only_model (bool): When resuming from a checkpoint, whether to load only the model weights and not the
73	            optimizer/scheduler states. Defaults to False.
74	
75	        optimizer (Optional[str]):The optimizer plugin to use (takes priority over `--optim`), default is None.
76	            Available optimizers can be found in `optimizers/mapping.py`
77	        loss_type (Optional[str]): Custom loss_type name. Default is None, uses the model's built-in loss function.
78	            Available loss options can be found in `loss/mapping.py`
79	        metric (Optional[str]): Custom eval metric name. Default is None. Available eval_metric options can be found
80	            in `metrics/mapping.py`.
81	        callbacks (List[str]): Custom trainer callbacks, default is `[]`. Available callbacks can be found
82	            in `callbacks/mapping.py`.
83	        early_stop_interval (Optional[int]): The interval for early stopping. Training will be terminated if the
84	            `best_metric` does not improve for `early_stop_interval` evaluation periods (based on `save_steps`). It is
85	            recommended to set `eval_steps` and `save_steps` to the same value. The implementation can be found in the
86	            callback plugin. For more complex requirements, you can directly override the implementation in
87	            `callback.py`. Defaults to None.
88	
89	        eval_use_evalscope (bool): Whether to use EvalScope for evaluation during training. Must be set to `True` to
90	            enable it. Refer to examples for usage details. Defaults to False.
91	        eval_dataset (List[str]): A list of evaluation dataset names. Multiple datasets can be specified, separated
92	            by spaces.
93	        eval_dataset_args (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Arguments for the evaluation dataset(s), provided as a JSON
94	            string or a dictionary.
95	        eval_limit (Optional[int]): The maximum number of samples to use from the evaluation dataset. Defaults to None.
96	        eval_generation_config (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Model inference configuration for evaluation, provided as
97	            a JSON string or a dictionary, e.g., `{'max_tokens': 512}`. Defaults to None.
98	        extra_eval_args (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Extra arguments for evaluation, provided as a JSON string or a
99	            dictionary.
100	
101	        use_galore (bool): Flag to indicate if Galore is used. Default is False.
102	        galore_target_modules (Optional[List[str]]): List of target modules for Galore. Default is None.
103	        galore_rank (int): Rank for Galore. Default is 128.
104	        galore_update_proj_gap (int): Update projection gap for Galore. Default is 50.
105	        galore_scale (float): Scaling factor for Galore. Default is 1.0.
106	        galore_proj_type (str): Projection type for Galore. Default is 'std'.
107	        galore_optim_per_parameter (bool): Flag to indicate if optimization is per parameter for Galore.
108	            Default is False.
109	        galore_with_embedding (bool): Flag to indicate if embedding is used with Galore. Default is False.
110	        galore_quantization (bool): Flag to indicate if use Q-Galore. Default is False.
111	        galore_proj_quant (bool): Flag to indicate if projection quantization is used for Galore. Default is False.
112	        galore_proj_bits (int): Number of bits for projection quantization. Default is 4.
113	        galore_proj_group_size (int): Group size for projection quantization. Default is 256.
114	        galore_cos_threshold (float): Cosine threshold for projection quantization. Default is 0.4.
115	        galore_gamma_proj (int): Gamma for projection quantization. Default is 2.
116	        galore_queue_size (int): Queue size for projection quantization. Default is 5.
117	        lisa_activated_layers (int): Number of activated layers for LISA. Default is 0.
118	        lisa_step_interval (int): Step interval for LISA activation. Default is 20.
119	
120	        use_flash_ckpt (bool): Whether to enable DLRover Flash Checkpoint. When enabled, weights are first saved to
121	            shared memory and then asynchronously persisted to disk. Currently does not support the safetensors format.
122	            It is recommended to use this with `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF="expandable_segments:True"` to prevent CUDA OOM
123	            errors during training. Defaults to False.
124	    """
125	    per_device_train_batch_size: int = 1
126	    per_device_eval_batch_size: int = 1
127	    gradient_accumulation_steps: Optional[int] = None
128	    tuner_backend: Optional[str] = None
129	
130	    gradient_checkpointing: bool = True
131	    vit_gradient_checkpointing: Optional[bool] = None
132	    gradient_checkpointing_kwargs: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
133	    logging_first_step: bool = True
134	    logging_steps: int = 5
135	    router_aux_loss_coef: float = 0.
136	    enable_dft_loss: bool = False  # https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.05629
137	    enable_channel_loss: bool = False
138	    safe_serialization: bool = True
139	    max_shard_size: str = '5GB'
140	
141	    weight_decay: float = 0.1
142	    adam_beta2: float = 0.95
143	    lr_scheduler_type: str = 'cosine'
144	    lr_scheduler_kwargs: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
145	    report_to: List[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: ['tensorboard'])
146	    dataloader_num_workers: Optional[int] = None
147	    dataloader_persistent_workers: bool = False
148	    dataloader_prefetch_factor: Optional[int] = None
149	    use_liger_kernel: bool = False
150	
151	    # extra
152	    check_model: bool = True
153	    acc_strategy: Literal['token', 'seq'] = 'token'
154	    train_dataloader_shuffle: bool = True
155	    group_by_length: bool = False
156	    max_epochs: Optional[int] = None
157	    aligner_lr: Optional[float] = None
158	    vit_lr: Optional[float] = None
159	    use_logits_to_keep: Optional[bool] = None
160	    ds3_gather_for_generation: bool = True
161	    resume_only_model: bool = False
162	
163	    # plugins
164	    optimizer: Optional[str] = None
165	    loss_type: Optional[str] = field(default=None, metadata={'help': f'loss_func choices: {list(loss_map.keys())}'})
166	    # embedding (Matryoshka Representation Learning)
167	    # Dict[int, float], where the key is the embedding dimension and the value is the corresponding loss weight,
168	    # e.g. '{"32": 1.0, "64": 1.0, "128": 1.0}'.
169	    mrl_dims: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
170	    eval_metric: Optional[str] = None
171	    callbacks: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
172	    # early_step
173	    early_stop_interval: Optional[int] = None
174	
175	    # train-eval loop args
176	    eval_use_evalscope: bool = False
177	    eval_dataset: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
178	    eval_dataset_args: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
179	    eval_limit: Optional[int] = None
180	    eval_generation_config: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
181	    extra_eval_args: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
182	
183	    # Value copied from SftArguments
184	    tuner_type: Optional[str] = None
185	
186	    # galore
187	    use_galore: bool = False
188	    galore_target_modules: Optional[List[str]] = None
189	    galore_rank: int = 128
190	    galore_update_proj_gap: int = 50
191	    galore_scale: float = 1.0
192	    galore_proj_type: str = 'std'
193	    galore_optim_per_parameter: bool = False
194	    galore_with_embedding: bool = False
195	    galore_quantization: bool = False
196	    galore_proj_quant: bool = False
197	    galore_proj_bits: int = 4
198	    galore_proj_group_size: int = 256
199	    galore_cos_threshold: float = 0.4
200	    galore_gamma_proj: int = 2
201	    galore_queue_size: int = 5
202	    # lisa
203	    lisa_activated_layers: int = 0
204	    lisa_step_interval: int = 20
205	
206	    # dlrover flash_checkpoint
207	    use_flash_ckpt: bool = False
208	
209	    @staticmethod
210	    def _patch_liger_kernel():
211	        # fix logits_to_keep
212	        from liger_kernel.transformers.model import loss_utils
213	        origin_LigerForCausalLMLoss = loss_utils.LigerForCausalLMLoss
214	
215	        def LigerForCausalLMLoss(hidden_states, *args, **kwargs):
216	            hidden_states = hidden_states.contiguous()
217	            for key in ['cu_seq_lens_q', 'cu_seq_lens_k', 'max_length_q', 'max_length_k']:
218	                kwargs.pop(key, None)
219	            return origin_LigerForCausalLMLoss(hidden_states, *args, **kwargs)
220	
221	        loss_utils.LigerForCausalLMLoss = LigerForCausalLMLoss
222	
223	    def _init_liger(self):
224	        if self.use_liger_kernel:
225	            assert is_liger_available(), 'use_liger_kernel requires liger_kernels, try `pip install liger-kernel`'
226	            try:
227	                self._patch_liger_kernel()
228	            except Exception:
229	                logger.warning('Failed to patch liger_kernel')
230	
231	    def _init_callbacks(self):
232	        if self.lisa_activated_layers > 0:
233	            self.callbacks.append('lisa')
234	        if self.tuner_type == 'adalora':
235	            self.callbacks.append('adalora')
236	        if self.early_stop_interval is not None and self.early_stop_interval > 0:
237	            self.callbacks.append('early_stop')
238	        fsdp_config = getattr(self, 'fsdp_config', {})
239	        if isinstance(fsdp_config, dict) and fsdp_config.get('activation_cpu_offload', False):
240	            self.callbacks.append('activation_cpu_offload')
241	
242	    def __post_init__(self):
243	        if hasattr(self, 'output_dir'):
244	            self.output_dir = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(self.output_dir))
245	        if is_mp() and self.use_liger_kernel:
246	            raise ValueError('liger_kernel does not support device_map. '
247	                             'Please use DDP/DeepSpeed for multi-GPU training.')
248	
249	        if self.optimizer is None and (self.vit_lr is not None or self.aligner_lr is not None):
250	            self.optimizer = 'multimodal'
251	        self._init_callbacks()
252	        if self.gradient_accumulation_steps is None:
253	            world_size = get_dist_setting()[2]
254	            self.gradient_accumulation_steps = max(1, math.ceil(16 / self.per_device_train_batch_size / world_size))
255	            logger.info(f'Setting args.gradient_accumulation_steps: {self.gradient_accumulation_steps}')
256	        if self.lr_scheduler_kwargs:
257	            self.lr_scheduler_kwargs = json_parse_to_dict(self.lr_scheduler_kwargs)
258	        if 'wandb' in self.report_to:
259	            os.environ.setdefault('WANDB_PROJECT', 'ms-swift')
260	        if self.vit_gradient_checkpointing is None:
261	            self.vit_gradient_checkpointing = self.gradient_checkpointing
262	        if self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs:
263	            self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs = json_parse_to_dict(self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs)
264	        if self.mrl_dims is not None:
265	            self.mrl_dims = json_parse_to_dict(self.mrl_dims)
266	            self.mrl_dims = {int(k): float(v) for k, v in self.mrl_dims.items()}
267	        self._init_liger()
268	        if self.dataloader_num_workers is None:
269	            if platform.system() == 'Windows':
270	                self.dataloader_num_workers = 0
271	            else:
272	                self.dataloader_num_workers = 1
273	            logger.info(f'Setting args.dataloader_num_workers: {self.dataloader_num_workers}')
274	        if self.dataloader_prefetch_factor is None and self.dataloader_num_workers > 0:
275	            self.dataloader_prefetch_factor = 2
276	        if self.eval_use_evalscope:
277	            try:
278	                import evalscope
279	            except ImportError:
280	                raise ImportError('evalscope is not installed, please install it by `pip install evalscope`')
281	            self.eval_dataset_args = json_parse_to_dict(self.eval_dataset_args)
282	            self.eval_generation_config = json_parse_to_dict(self.eval_generation_config)
283	            self.extra_eval_args = json_parse_to_dict(self.extra_eval_args)
284	
285	
286	@dataclass
287	class TrainingArguments(TrainArgumentsMixin, HfTrainingArguments):
288	
289	    def __post_init__(self):
290	        TrainArgumentsMixin.__post_init__(self)
291	        HfTrainingArguments.__post_init__(self)
292	
293	
294	@dataclass
295	class Seq2SeqTrainingArguments(TrainArgumentsMixin, HfSeq2SeqTrainingArguments):
296	
297	    def __post_init__(self):
298	        TrainArgumentsMixin.__post_init__(self)
299	        HfSeq2SeqTrainingArguments.__post_init__(self)

# Copyright (c) ModelScope Contributors. All rights reserved.
import math
import os
import platform
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from transformers.training_args import TrainingArguments as HfTrainingArguments
from transformers.training_args_seq2seq import Seq2SeqTrainingArguments as HfSeq2SeqTrainingArguments
from typing import Dict, List, Literal, Optional, Union

from swift.loss import loss_map
from swift.utils import get_dist_setting, get_logger, is_liger_available, is_mp, json_parse_to_dict

logger = get_logger()


@dataclass
class TrainArgumentsMixin:
    """A dataclass mixin for configuring model training parameters.

    Args:
        per_device_train_batch_size (int): The batch size per GPU/TPU core for training. Defaults to 1.
        per_device_eval_batch_size (int): The batch size per GPU/TPU core for evaluation. Defaults to 1.
        gradient_accumulation_steps (Optional[int]): The number of update steps to accumulate gradients for before
            performing an optimizer step.
        tuner_backend (Optional[str]): The backend to use for parameter-efficient fine-tuning (e.g., 'peft'). Defaults
            to None.
        gradient_checkpointing (bool): If True, use gradient checkpointing to save memory at the cost of a slower
            backward pass. Defaults to True.
        vit_gradient_checkpointing (Optional[bool]): A specific gradient checkpointing setting for the Vision
            Transformer part of the model. Defaults to None.
        gradient_checkpointing_kwargs (Optional[Union[dict, str]]): Keyword arguments for
            `torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint`. Defaults to None.
        logging_first_step (bool): Whether to log the first global step. Defaults to True.
        logging_steps (int): Log every `logging_steps` global steps. Defaults to 5.
        router_aux_loss_coef (float): The coefficient for the router auxiliary loss in Mixture-of-Experts models.
            Defaults to 0.0.
        enable_dft_loss (bool): Whether to enable Diversity-from-Diversity (DFD) loss.
            See https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.05629. Defaults to False.
        enable_channel_loss (bool): Whether to enable channel loss. Defaults to False.
        weight_decay (float): The weight decay to apply (if not zero) to all layers except bias and LayerNorm weights.
            Defaults to 0.1.
        adam_beta2 (float): The beta2 hyperparameter for the AdamW optimizer. Defaults to 0.95.
        lr_scheduler_type (str): The learning rate scheduler type to use. Defaults to 'cosine'.
        lr_scheduler_kwargs (Optional[Union[dict, str]]): Additional keyword arguments for the learning rate scheduler,
            passed as a JSON string or a dictionary. Defaults to None.
        report_to (List[str]): The list of integrations to report results to (e.g., 'tensorboard', 'wandb'). Defaults
            to ['tensorboard']. If you specify `--report_to wandb`, you can set the project name through `WANDB_PROJECT`
            and specify the API KEY corresponding to your account through `WANDB_API_KEY`.
        dataloader_num_workers (Optional[int]): The number of subprocesses to use for data loading. Defaults to None.
        dataloader_persistent_workers (bool): If True, the data loader workers will not be shut down after a dataset
            has been consumed once. Defaults to False.
        dataloader_prefetch_factor (Optional[int]): The number of batches loaded in advance by each worker. Defaults
            to None.
        use_liger_kernel (bool): Whether to use the Liger kernel for optimization. Defaults to False.
        check_model (bool): If True, checks local model files for corruption or modification and provides a warning.
            Should be set to False in an offline environment. Defaults to True.
        acc_strategy (Literal['token', 'seq']): The strategy for calculating accuracy during training and validation.
            Can be 'token' for token-level accuracy or 'seq' for sequence-level accuracy. Defaults to 'token'.
        train_dataloader_shuffle (bool): Whether to shuffle the training data. Defaults to True.
        group_by_length (bool): Whether to group samples with approximately the same length together in the
            training dataset (with a random factor).
        max_epochs (Optional[int]): The total number of training epochs to perform. Overrides `num_train_epochs`.
            Defaults to None.
        aligner_lr (Optional[float]): A specific learning rate for the aligner part of the model. Defaults to None.
        vit_lr (Optional[float]): A specific learning rate for the Vision Transformer part of the model. Defaults to
            None.
        use_logits_to_keep (Optional[bool]): If enabled, reduces VRAM usage and speeds up training by calculating and
            storing only the necessary logits based on the labels during the forward pass. If None, the behavior is
            automatically determined. Defaults to None.
        ds3_gather_for_generation (bool): In DeepSpeed ZeRO-3, whether to gather model parameters for generation.
            Defaults to True.
        resume_only_model (bool): When resuming from a checkpoint, whether to load only the model weights and not the
            optimizer/scheduler states. Defaults to False.

        optimizer (Optional[str]):The optimizer plugin to use (takes priority over `--optim`), default is None.
            Available optimizers can be found in `optimizers/mapping.py`
        loss_type (Optional[str]): Custom loss_type name. Default is None, uses the model's built-in loss function.
            Available loss options can be found in `loss/mapping.py`
        metric (Optional[str]): Custom eval metric name. Default is None. Available eval_metric options can be found
            in `metrics/mapping.py`.
        callbacks (List[str]): Custom trainer callbacks, default is `[]`. Available callbacks can be found
            in `callbacks/mapping.py`.
        early_stop_interval (Optional[int]): The interval for early stopping. Training will be terminated if the
            `best_metric` does not improve for `early_stop_interval` evaluation periods (based on `save_steps`). It is
            recommended to set `eval_steps` and `save_steps` to the same value. The implementation can be found in the
            callback plugin. For more complex requirements, you can directly override the implementation in
            `callback.py`. Defaults to None.

        eval_use_evalscope (bool): Whether to use EvalScope for evaluation during training. Must be set to `True` to
            enable it. Refer to examples for usage details. Defaults to False.
        eval_dataset (List[str]): A list of evaluation dataset names. Multiple datasets can be specified, separated
            by spaces.
        eval_dataset_args (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Arguments for the evaluation dataset(s), provided as a JSON
            string or a dictionary.
        eval_limit (Optional[int]): The maximum number of samples to use from the evaluation dataset. Defaults to None.
        eval_generation_config (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Model inference configuration for evaluation, provided as
            a JSON string or a dictionary, e.g., `{'max_tokens': 512}`. Defaults to None.
        extra_eval_args (Optional[Union[str, dict]]): Extra arguments for evaluation, provided as a JSON string or a
            dictionary.

        use_galore (bool): Flag to indicate if Galore is used. Default is False.
        galore_target_modules (Optional[List[str]]): List of target modules for Galore. Default is None.
        galore_rank (int): Rank for Galore. Default is 128.
        galore_update_proj_gap (int): Update projection gap for Galore. Default is 50.
        galore_scale (float): Scaling factor for Galore. Default is 1.0.
        galore_proj_type (str): Projection type for Galore. Default is 'std'.
        galore_optim_per_parameter (bool): Flag to indicate if optimization is per parameter for Galore.
            Default is False.
        galore_with_embedding (bool): Flag to indicate if embedding is used with Galore. Default is False.
        galore_quantization (bool): Flag to indicate if use Q-Galore. Default is False.
        galore_proj_quant (bool): Flag to indicate if projection quantization is used for Galore. Default is False.
        galore_proj_bits (int): Number of bits for projection quantization. Default is 4.
        galore_proj_group_size (int): Group size for projection quantization. Default is 256.
        galore_cos_threshold (float): Cosine threshold for projection quantization. Default is 0.4.
        galore_gamma_proj (int): Gamma for projection quantization. Default is 2.
        galore_queue_size (int): Queue size for projection quantization. Default is 5.
        lisa_activated_layers (int): Number of activated layers for LISA. Default is 0.
        lisa_step_interval (int): Step interval for LISA activation. Default is 20.

        use_flash_ckpt (bool): Whether to enable DLRover Flash Checkpoint. When enabled, weights are first saved to
            shared memory and then asynchronously persisted to disk. Currently does not support the safetensors format.
            It is recommended to use this with `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF="expandable_segments:True"` to prevent CUDA OOM
            errors during training. Defaults to False.
    """
    per_device_train_batch_size: int = 1
    per_device_eval_batch_size: int = 1
    gradient_accumulation_steps: Optional[int] = None
    tuner_backend: Optional[str] = None

    gradient_checkpointing: bool = True
    vit_gradient_checkpointing: Optional[bool] = None
    gradient_checkpointing_kwargs: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
    logging_first_step: bool = True
    logging_steps: int = 5
    router_aux_loss_coef: float = 0.
    enable_dft_loss: bool = False  # https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.05629
    enable_channel_loss: bool = False
    safe_serialization: bool = True
    max_shard_size: str = '5GB'

    weight_decay: float = 0.1
    adam_beta2: float = 0.95
    lr_scheduler_type: str = 'cosine'
    lr_scheduler_kwargs: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
    report_to: List[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: ['tensorboard'])
    dataloader_num_workers: Optional[int] = None
    dataloader_persistent_workers: bool = False
    dataloader_prefetch_factor: Optional[int] = None
    use_liger_kernel: bool = False

    # extra
    check_model: bool = True
    acc_strategy: Literal['token', 'seq'] = 'token'
    train_dataloader_shuffle: bool = True
    group_by_length: bool = False
    max_epochs: Optional[int] = None
    aligner_lr: Optional[float] = None
    vit_lr: Optional[float] = None
    use_logits_to_keep: Optional[bool] = None
    ds3_gather_for_generation: bool = True
    resume_only_model: bool = False

    # plugins
    optimizer: Optional[str] = None
    loss_type: Optional[str] = field(default=None, metadata={'help': f'loss_func choices: {list(loss_map.keys())}'})
    # embedding (Matryoshka Representation Learning)
    # Dict[int, float], where the key is the embedding dimension and the value is the corresponding loss weight,
    # e.g. '{"32": 1.0, "64": 1.0, "128": 1.0}'.
    mrl_dims: Optional[Union[dict, str]] = None
    eval_metric: Optional[str] = None
    callbacks: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # early_step
    early_stop_interval: Optional[int] = None

    # train-eval loop args
    eval_use_evalscope: bool = False
    eval_dataset: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    eval_dataset_args: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
    eval_limit: Optional[int] = None
    eval_generation_config: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
    extra_eval_args: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None

    # Value copied from SftArguments
    tuner_type: Optional[str] = None

    # galore
    use_galore: bool = False
    galore_target_modules: Optional[List[str]] = None
    galore_rank: int = 128
    galore_update_proj_gap: int = 50
    galore_scale: float = 1.0
    galore_proj_type: str = 'std'
    galore_optim_per_parameter: bool = False
    galore_with_embedding: bool = False
    galore_quantization: bool = False
    galore_proj_quant: bool = False
    galore_proj_bits: int = 4
    galore_proj_group_size: int = 256
    galore_cos_threshold: float = 0.4
    galore_gamma_proj: int = 2
    galore_queue_size: int = 5
    # lisa
    lisa_activated_layers: int = 0
    lisa_step_interval: int = 20

    # dlrover flash_checkpoint
    use_flash_ckpt: bool = False

    @staticmethod
    def _patch_liger_kernel():
        # fix logits_to_keep
        from liger_kernel.transformers.model import loss_utils
        origin_LigerForCausalLMLoss = loss_utils.LigerForCausalLMLoss

        def LigerForCausalLMLoss(hidden_states, *args, **kwargs):
            hidden_states = hidden_states.contiguous()
            for key in ['cu_seq_lens_q', 'cu_seq_lens_k', 'max_length_q', 'max_length_k']:
                kwargs.pop(key, None)
            return origin_LigerForCausalLMLoss(hidden_states, *args, **kwargs)

        loss_utils.LigerForCausalLMLoss = LigerForCausalLMLoss

    def _init_liger(self):
        if self.use_liger_kernel:
            assert is_liger_available(), 'use_liger_kernel requires liger_kernels, try `pip install liger-kernel`'
            try:
                self._patch_liger_kernel()
            except Exception:
                logger.warning('Failed to patch liger_kernel')

    def _init_callbacks(self):
        if self.lisa_activated_layers > 0:
            self.callbacks.append('lisa')
        if self.tuner_type == 'adalora':
            self.callbacks.append('adalora')
        if self.early_stop_interval is not None and self.early_stop_interval > 0:
            self.callbacks.append('early_stop')
        fsdp_config = getattr(self, 'fsdp_config', {})
        if isinstance(fsdp_config, dict) and fsdp_config.get('activation_cpu_offload', False):
            self.callbacks.append('activation_cpu_offload')

    def __post_init__(self):
        if hasattr(self, 'output_dir'):
            self.output_dir = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(self.output_dir))
        if is_mp() and self.use_liger_kernel:
            raise ValueError('liger_kernel does not support device_map. '
                             'Please use DDP/DeepSpeed for multi-GPU training.')

        if self.optimizer is None and (self.vit_lr is not None or self.aligner_lr is not None):
            self.optimizer = 'multimodal'
        self._init_callbacks()
        if self.gradient_accumulation_steps is None:
            world_size = get_dist_setting()[2]
            self.gradient_accumulation_steps = max(1, math.ceil(16 / self.per_device_train_batch_size / world_size))
            logger.info(f'Setting args.gradient_accumulation_steps: {self.gradient_accumulation_steps}')
        if self.lr_scheduler_kwargs:
            self.lr_scheduler_kwargs = json_parse_to_dict(self.lr_scheduler_kwargs)
        if 'wandb' in self.report_to:
            os.environ.setdefault('WANDB_PROJECT', 'ms-swift')
        if self.vit_gradient_checkpointing is None:
            self.vit_gradient_checkpointing = self.gradient_checkpointing
        if self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs:
            self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs = json_parse_to_dict(self.gradient_checkpointing_kwargs)
        if self.mrl_dims is not None:
            self.mrl_dims = json_parse_to_dict(self.mrl_dims)
            self.mrl_dims = {int(k): float(v) for k, v in self.mrl_dims.items()}
        self._init_liger()
        if self.dataloader_num_workers is None:
            if platform.system() == 'Windows':
                self.dataloader_num_workers = 0
            else:
                self.dataloader_num_workers = 1
            logger.info(f'Setting args.dataloader_num_workers: {self.dataloader_num_workers}')
        if self.dataloader_prefetch_factor is None and self.dataloader_num_workers > 0:
            self.dataloader_prefetch_factor = 2
        if self.eval_use_evalscope:
            try:
                import evalscope
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError('evalscope is not installed, please install it by `pip install evalscope`')
            self.eval_dataset_args = json_parse_to_dict(self.eval_dataset_args)
            self.eval_generation_config = json_parse_to_dict(self.eval_generation_config)
            self.extra_eval_args = json_parse_to_dict(self.extra_eval_args)


@dataclass
class TrainingArguments(TrainArgumentsMixin, HfTrainingArguments):

    def __post_init__(self):
        TrainArgumentsMixin.__post_init__(self)
        HfTrainingArguments.__post_init__(self)


@dataclass
class Seq2SeqTrainingArguments(TrainArgumentsMixin, HfSeq2SeqTrainingArguments):

    def __post_init__(self):
        TrainArgumentsMixin.__post_init__(self)
        HfSeq2SeqTrainingArguments.__post_init__(self)

1	import os
2	import sys
3	
4	from swift.utils import git_clone_github
5	from .base import OptimizerCallback
6	
7	
8	class MuonOptimizerCallback(OptimizerCallback):
9	
10	    def create_optimizer(self, model=None):
11	        args = self.args
12	        if model is None:
13	            model = self.trainer.model
14	        if not args.local_repo_path:
15	            args.local_repo_path = git_clone_github('https://github.com/MoonshotAI/Moonlight.git')
16	        sys.path.append(os.path.join(args.local_repo_path, 'examples'))
17	        from toy_train import Muon
18	
19	        # parse args.optim_args
20	        optim_args = {}
21	        if args.optim_args:
22	            for mapping in args.optim_args.replace(' ', '').split(','):
23	                key, value = mapping.split('=')
24	                optim_args[key] = value
25	
26	        model_arch = model.model_meta.model_arch
27	        embed_key = getattr(model_arch, 'embedding', None) or 'embed_tokens'
28	        lm_head_key = getattr(model_arch, 'lm_head', None) or 'lm_head'
29	        muon_params = [
30	            p for n, p in model.named_parameters()
31	            if p.requires_grad and p.ndim >= 2 and embed_key not in n and lm_head_key not in n
32	        ]
33	        adamw_params = [
34	            p for n, p in model.named_parameters()
35	            if p.requires_grad and not (p.ndim >= 2 and embed_key not in n and lm_head_key not in n)
36	        ]
37	
38	        return Muon(
39	            lr=args.learning_rate,
40	            wd=args.weight_decay,
41	            muon_params=muon_params,
42	            adamw_params=adamw_params,
43	            adamw_betas=(args.adam_beta1, args.adam_beta2),
44	            adamw_eps=args.adam_epsilon,
45	            **optim_args,
46	        )

11	        if model is None:
12	            model = self.trainer.model
13	        optimizer_grouped_parameters = None
14	        if hasattr(model, 'create_optimizer_param_groups'):
15	            # Lora+ parameter groups

17	                lr=args.learning_rate, weight_decay=args.weight_decay)
18

20	            # Default parameter groups
21	            decay_parameters = HfTrainer.get_decay_parameter_names(None, model)
22	            optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
23	                {
24	                    'params': [p for n, p in model.named_parameters() if (n in decay_parameters and p.requires_grad)],
25	                    'weight_decay': args.weight_decay,
26	                },
27	                {
28	                    'params':
29	                    [p for n, p in model.named_parameters() if (n not in decay_parameters and p.requires_grad)],
30	                    'weight_decay': 0.0,
31	                },
32	            ]
33	        optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = HfTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(args)
34	        return optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)

6	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

9	
10	logger = get_logger()
11	
12	
13	class Metric(ABC):
14	
15	    def __init__(self):
16	        self._default = {}
17	        self._default_factory = {}
18	
19	    def add_state(self, name: str, default=None, default_factory=None) -> None:
20	        if not hasattr(self, '_default'):
21	            raise AttributeError('Please call super().__init__() first.')
22	        if default is None:
23	            self._default_factory[name] = default_factory
24	            assert name not in self._default, f'self._default: {self._default}'
25	            default = default_factory()
26	        else:
27	            self._default[name] = default
28	            assert name not in self._default_factory, f'self._default_factory: {self._default_factory}'
29	        setattr(self, name, default)
30	
31	    def reset(self):
32	        for k, v in self._default.items():
33	            setattr(self, k, v)
34	        for k, v in self._default_factory.items():
35	            setattr(self, k, v())
36	
37	    @abstractmethod
38	    def update(self, *args, **kwargs):
39	        pass
40	
41	    @abstractmethod
42	    def compute(self):
43	        pass
44	
45	
46	class InferStats(Metric):
47	
48	    def __init__(self):
49	        super().__init__()
50	        self.add_state('start_runtime', default_factory=lambda: time.perf_counter())
51	        self.add_state('num_prompt_tokens', default_factory=dict)
52	        self.add_state('num_generated_tokens', default_factory=dict)
53	
54	    def update(self, output):
55	        id_ = output.id
56	        self.num_prompt_tokens[id_] = output.usage.prompt_tokens
57	        self.num_generated_tokens[id_] = output.usage.completion_tokens
58	
59	    def compute(self):
60	        runtime = time.perf_counter() - self.start_runtime
61	        num_samples = len(self.num_generated_tokens)
62	        num_generated_tokens = sum(self.num_generated_tokens.values())
63	        return {
64	            'num_prompt_tokens': sum(self.num_prompt_tokens.values()),
65	            'num_generated_tokens': num_generated_tokens,
66	            'num_samples': num_samples,
67	            'runtime': runtime,
68	            'samples/s': num_samples / runtime,
69	            'tokens/s': num_generated_tokens / runtime,
70	        }
71	
72	
73	class MeanMetric(Metric):
74	
75	    def __init__(self, nan_value=0, device=None, group=None):
76	        super().__init__()
77	        self.nan_value = nan_value
78	        self.add_state('state', default=0.)
79	        self.add_state('count', default=0)
80	        if device is None:
81	            device = get_current_device()
82	        self.device = device
83	        self.group = group
84	
85	    def update(self, state: torch.Tensor):
86	        if isinstance(state, (torch.Tensor, np.ndarray)):
87	            if state.ndim == 0:
88	                count = 1
89	                state = state.item()
90	            else:
91	                count = state.shape[0]
92	                state = state.sum().item()
93	        elif isinstance(state, (list, tuple)):
94	            count = len(state)
95	            state = sum(state)
96	        else:
97	            count = 1
98	
99	        self.state += state
100	        self.count += count
101	
102	    def compute(self):
103	        if dist.is_initialized():
104	            tensor = torch.tensor([self.state, self.count], dtype=torch.float32, device=self.device)
105	            dist.all_reduce(tensor, op=dist.ReduceOp.SUM, group=self.group)
106	            self.state, self.count = tensor[0].item(), int(tensor[1].item())
107	        if self.count == 0:
108	            value = self.nan_value
109	        else:
110	            value = self.state / self.count
111	        return {
112	            'value': value,
113	        }

13	def get_param_startswith(model,
14	                         chosen_prefix: List[str],
15	                         rejected_prefix: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> List[Tuple[str, nn.Parameter]]:
16	    chosen_prefix = chosen_prefix or []
17	    rejected_prefix = rejected_prefix or []
18	    res = []
19	    if not chosen_prefix:
20	        return res
21	    is_peft_model = isinstance(model, PeftModel)
22	    if is_peft_model:
23	        model = model.model
24	    for n, p in model.named_parameters():
25	        if not p.requires_grad:
26	            continue
27	        is_rejected = False
28	        for prefix in rejected_prefix:
29	            if n.startswith(prefix):
30	                is_rejected = True
31	                break
32	        if is_rejected:
33	            continue
34	        for prefix in chosen_prefix:
35	            if n.startswith(prefix):
36	                if is_peft_model:
37	                    n = f'base_model.model.{n}'
38	                res.append((n, p))
39	                break
40	    return res

43	class MultimodalOptimizerCallback(OptimizerCallback):
44	
45	    def create_optimizer(self, model=None):
46	        """ViT/Aligner/LLM use different learning rates."""
47	        args = self.args
48	        if model is None:
49	            model = self.trainer.model
50	        decay_parameters = set(HfTrainer.get_decay_parameter_names(None, model))
51	        model_arch = model.model_meta.model_arch
52	        vit_parameters = get_param_startswith(model, model_arch.vision_tower, model_arch.aligner)
53	        aligner_parameters = get_param_startswith(model, model_arch.aligner)
54	        llm_parameters = get_param_startswith(model, model_arch.language_model)
55	        optimizer_grouped_parameters = []
56	        vit_lr = args.vit_lr if args.vit_lr is not None else args.learning_rate
57	        aligner_lr = args.aligner_lr if args.aligner_lr is not None else args.learning_rate
58	        logger.info(f'vit_lr: {vit_lr}, aligner_lr: {aligner_lr}, llm_lr: {args.learning_rate}')
59	        for lr, parameters in zip([vit_lr, aligner_lr, args.learning_rate],
60	                                  [vit_parameters, aligner_parameters, llm_parameters]):
61	            for use_wd, wd in zip([False, True], [0., args.weight_decay]):
62	                if use_wd:
63	                    params = [p for n, p in parameters if n in decay_parameters]
64	                else:
65	                    params = [p for n, p in parameters if n not in decay_parameters]
66	                if not params:
67	                    continue
68	                optimizer_grouped_parameters.append({
69	                    'params': params,
70	                    'weight_decay': wd,
71	                    'lr': lr,
72	                })
73	        optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = HfTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(args, model)
74	        return optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)

# Copyright (c) ModelScope Contributors. All rights reserved.
import numpy as np
import torch
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from .base import TrainerCallback

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from swift.trainers import Trainer, TrainingArguments


class LISACallback(TrainerCallback):

    def __init__(self, args: 'TrainingArguments', trainer: 'Trainer'):
        assert args.tuner_type == 'full', 'LISA only supports full parameter training.'
        super().__init__(args, trainer)
        self.n_layers = args.lisa_activated_layers
        self.step_interval = args.lisa_step_interval
        self.model = self.trainer.model
        layers_name = None
        layers = None
        for name, module in self.model.named_modules():
            if isinstance(module, torch.nn.ModuleList):
                layers_name = name
                layers = module
                break
        assert layers_name is not None
        self.layers_attribute = layers_name
        self.total_layers = len(layers)

        # Freeze all layers upon initialization
        self.freeze_all_layers()
        self.active_layers_indices = []
        self.switch_active_layers()

    def freeze_all_layers(self):
        layers = self.model.get_submodule(self.layers_attribute)
        for layer in layers:
            for param in layer.parameters():
                param.requires_grad = False

    def on_step_begin(self, args, state, control, **kwargs):
        # Check if it's time to switch active layers, including at step 0
        if state.global_step % self.step_interval == 0 or state.global_step == 1:
            self.switch_active_layers()

    def switch_active_layers(self):
        # First, disable gradients for all layers
        self.freeze_all_layers()

        # Randomly select n_layers to activate
        layers = self.model.get_submodule(self.layers_attribute)
        self.active_layers_indices = np.random.choice(range(self.total_layers), self.n_layers, replace=False)
        # Enable gradients only for the selected layers
        for idx in self.active_layers_indices:
            for param in layers[idx].parameters():
                param.requires_grad = True

12	def compute_rouge_bleu(preds: List[str], labels: List[str]):
13	    import jieba
14	    from nltk.translate.bleu_score import SmoothingFunction, sentence_bleu
15	    from rouge.rouge import Rouge
16	    score_dict = {key: MeanMetric() for key in ['rouge-1', 'rouge-2', 'rouge-l', 'bleu-4']}
17	
18	    for pred, label in zip(preds, labels):
19	        hypothesis = [w.strip(' ') for w in jieba.cut(pred) if w.strip(' ')]
20	        reference = [w.strip(' ') for w in jieba.cut(label) if w.strip(' ')]
21	        if not hypothesis or not reference:
22	            continue
23	        rouge = Rouge()
24	        scores = rouge.get_scores(' '.join(hypothesis), ' '.join(reference))[0]
25	        for k, v in scores.items():
26	            score_dict[k].update(v['f'])
27	        bleu_score = sentence_bleu([reference], hypothesis, smoothing_function=SmoothingFunction().method3)
28	        score_dict['bleu-4'].update(bleu_score)
29	
30	    return {k: round(v.compute()['value'] * 100, 6) for k, v in score_dict.items()}
31	
32	
33	class NlgMetrics(EvalMetrics):
34	
35	    def compute_metrics(self, eval_prediction: EvalPrediction) -> Dict[str, float]:
36	        # nlg: Natural Language Generation
37	        preds, labels = eval_prediction.predictions, eval_prediction.label_ids
38	        new_preds, new_labels = [], []
39	        for i in range(preds.shape[0]):
40	            new_preds.append(Serializer.from_tensor(preds[i]))
41	            new_labels.append(Serializer.from_tensor(labels[i]))
42	        return compute_rouge_bleu(new_preds, new_labels)
43

2	import importlib

15	try:
16	    from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import _LRScheduler as LRScheduler

23	logger = get_logger()

26	@dataclass
27	class GaLoreConfig:
28	    """
29	    The configuration class for the Galore module.
30	
31	
32	    See https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.03507
33	
34	    Args:
35	        rank (`int`): The galore rank
36	        target_modules (`Union[str, List[str]]`): The target modules to use, if `None`,
37	            will use all attn and mlp linears
38	        update_proj_gap(`int`): The projection update interval for galore
39	        proj_type(`str`) The project type of Galore, valid values are `std`,
40	            `reverse_std`, `right`, `left`, `full`
41	        galore_scale(float): the scale of gradient
42	        optim_per_parameter(bool): Gives one optimizer per parameter
43	    """
44	    rank: int = 128
45	    target_modules: Union[str, List[str]] = None
46	    update_proj_gap: int = 50
47	    galore_scale: float = 1.0
48	    proj_type: str = 'std'
49	    optim_per_parameter: bool = False
50	    quantize: bool = False
51	    proj_quant: bool = False
52	    proj_bits: int = 4
53	    proj_group_size: int = 256
54	    cos_threshold: float = 0.4
55	    gamma_proj: int = 2
56	    queue_size: int = 5
57	
58	
59	class GaloreOptimizerWrapper(Optimizer):
60	
61	    def __init__(self, optimizers: Dict[Any, Optimizer]):
62	        self.optimizers = optimizers
63	        super().__init__([torch.tensor([1., 2., 3.])], {'lr': 1.})
64	
65	    def zero_grad(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
66	        for optim in self.optimizers.values():
67	            optim.zero_grad(*args, **kwargs)
68	
69	    def step(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
70	        for optim in self.optimizers.values():
71	            optim.step(*args, **kwargs)
72	
73	
74	class GaloreSchedulerWrapper(LRScheduler):
75	
76	    def __init__(self, lr_schedulers: Dict[Any, LRScheduler]):
77	        self.lr_schedulers = lr_schedulers
78	
79	    def step(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
80	        for lr_scheduler in self.lr_schedulers.values():
81	            lr_scheduler.step(*args, **kwargs)
82	        self._last_lr = lr_scheduler.get_last_lr()
83	
84	
85	def _create_optimizer_and_scheduler(model: nn.Module, args: 'TrainingArguments', config: GaLoreConfig, max_steps,
86	                                    **defaults):
87	    galore_params = []
88	    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
89	        if not isinstance(module, (nn.Linear, nn.Embedding)) or \
90	                not any(target_key in module_name for target_key in config.target_modules):
91	            continue
92	
93	        if not module.weight.requires_grad:
94	            continue
95	
96	        logger.info(f'Enable GaLore for weights in module: {module_name}')
97	        galore_params.append(module.weight)
98	
99	    id_galore_params = [id(p) for p in galore_params]
100	    galore_defaults = {
101	        'rank': config.rank,
102	        'update_proj_gap': config.update_proj_gap,
103	        'scale': config.galore_scale,
104	        'proj_type': config.proj_type,
105	        **defaults
106	    }
107	    if config.quantize:
108	        galore_defaults['quant'] = config.proj_quant
109	        galore_defaults['quant_n_bit'] = config.proj_bits
110	        galore_defaults['quant_group_size'] = config.proj_group_size
111	        galore_defaults['cos_threshold'] = config.cos_threshold
112	        galore_defaults['gamma_proj'] = config.gamma_proj
113	        galore_defaults['queue_size'] = config.queue_size
114	    optim_cls, optim_kwargs = get_optimizer(args, config)
115	
116	    if config.optim_per_parameter and not config.quantize:
117	        # q-galore does not support optim_per_parameter
118	        optimizer_dict = {}
119	        galore_defaults['update_proj_gap'] = galore_defaults['update_proj_gap'] * 2
120	        for p in model.parameters():
121	            if p.requires_grad:
122	                if id(p) in id_galore_params:
123	                    optimizer_dict[p] = optim_cls([{'params': [p], **galore_defaults}], **optim_kwargs)
124	                else:
125	                    optimizer_dict[p] = optim_cls([{'params': [p], **defaults}], **optim_kwargs)
126	
127	        # get scheduler dict
128	        scheduler_dict = {}
129	        for p in model.parameters():
130	            if p.requires_grad:
131	                scheduler_dict[p] = get_scheduler(
132	                    optimizer=optimizer_dict[p],
133	                    name=args.lr_scheduler_type,
134	                    num_training_steps=max_steps * 2,
135	                    num_warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps * 2,
136	                    scheduler_specific_kwargs=args.lr_scheduler_kwargs,
137	                )
138	
139	        return GaloreOptimizerWrapper(optimizer_dict), GaloreSchedulerWrapper(scheduler_dict)
140	    else:
141	        decay_parameters = HfTrainer.get_decay_parameter_names(None, model)
142	        param_groups = [{
143	            'params': galore_params,
144	            **galore_defaults,
145	        }]
146	        param_groups.extend([
147	            {
148	                'params': [
149	                    p for n, p in model.named_parameters()
150	                    if (n in decay_parameters and id(p) not in id_galore_params and p.requires_grad)
151	                ],
152	                'weight_decay':
153	                defaults['weight_decay'],
154	            },
155	            {
156	                'params': [
157	                    p for n, p in model.named_parameters()
158	                    if (n not in decay_parameters and id(p) not in id_galore_params and p.requires_grad)
159	                ],
160	                'weight_decay':
161	                0.0,
162	            },
163	        ])
164	        optim = optim_cls(param_groups, **optim_kwargs)
165	        scheduler = get_scheduler(
166	            optimizer=optim,
167	            name=args.lr_scheduler_type,
168	            num_training_steps=max_steps,
169	            num_warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps,
170	            scheduler_specific_kwargs=args.lr_scheduler_kwargs,
171	        )
172	        return optim, scheduler
173	
174	
175	def get_optimizer(args: 'TrainingArguments', config: GaLoreConfig) -> Tuple[Any, Any]:
176	    # parse args.optim_args
177	    optim_args = {}
178	    if args.optim_args:
179	        for mapping in args.optim_args.replace(' ', '').split(','):
180	            key, value = mapping.split('=')
181	            optim_args[key] = value
182	
183	    optimizer_kwargs = {'lr': args.learning_rate}
184	
185	    adam_kwargs = {
186	        'betas': (args.adam_beta1, args.adam_beta2),
187	        'eps': args.adam_epsilon,
188	    }
189	    if args.optim == 'adafactor':
190	        from .adafactor import GaLoreAdafactor
191	        optimizer_cls = GaLoreAdafactor
192	        optimizer_kwargs.update({'scale_parameter': False, 'relative_step': False})
193	    elif args.optim in ('adamw_hf', 'adamw_torch', 'adamw_torch_fused'):
194	        if config.quantize:
195	            assert importlib.util.find_spec('q_galore_torch') is not None, \
196	                'Please install q-galore by `pip install q_galore_torch`'
197	            logger.info('If you encounter `absmax2` error, please downgrade your bitsandbytes to 0.40.0')
198	            from swift.utils import get_dist_setting
199	            _, _, world_size, _ = get_dist_setting()
200	            if world_size > 1:
201	                # from q_galore_torch import QGaLoreAdamW8bit_simulate as GaLoreAdamW
202	                from q_galore_torch import QGaLoreAdamW8bit as GaLoreAdamW
203	            else:
204	                from q_galore_torch import QGaLoreAdamW8bit as GaLoreAdamW
205	        else:
206	            from .adamw import GaLoreAdamW
207	        optimizer_cls = GaLoreAdamW
208	        optimizer_kwargs.update(adam_kwargs)
209	    elif 'adamw' in args.optim and '8bit' in args.optim:
210	        try:
211	            from .adamw8bit import GaLoreAdamW8bit
212	            optimizer_cls = GaLoreAdamW8bit
213	            optimizer_kwargs.update(adam_kwargs)
214	            optimizer_kwargs.update({'optim_bits': 8, 'is_paged': 'paged' in args.optim})
215	        except ImportError:
216	            raise ValueError('Trainer tried to instantiate bnb optimizer but bnb is not installed!')
217	    else:
218	        raise ValueError(f'Galore not supported for optimizer type: {args.optim}')
219	    return optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs
220	
221	
222	class GaloreOptimizerCallback(OptimizerCallback):
223	
224	    def create_optimizer_and_scheduler(self, num_training_steps: int):
225	        trainer = self.trainer
226	        args = self.args
227	        training_steps = calculate_max_steps(args, trainer.train_dataset)
228	        galore_config = GaLoreConfig(
229	            target_modules=args.galore_target_modules,
230	            rank=args.galore_rank,
231	            update_proj_gap=args.galore_update_proj_gap,
232	            galore_scale=args.galore_scale,
233	            proj_type=args.galore_proj_type,
234	            optim_per_parameter=args.galore_optim_per_parameter,
235	            quantize=args.galore_quantization,
236	            proj_quant=args.galore_proj_quant,
237	            proj_bits=args.galore_proj_bits,
238	            proj_group_size=args.galore_proj_group_size,
239	            cos_threshold=args.galore_cos_threshold,
240	            gamma_proj=args.galore_gamma_proj,
241	            queue_size=args.galore_queue_size,
242	        )
243	        optimizer, lr_scheduler = _create_optimizer_and_scheduler(
244	            trainer.model, args, galore_config, training_steps, lr=args.learning_rate, weight_decay=args.weight_decay)
245	        trainer.optimizer = optimizer
246	        trainer.lr_scheduler = lr_scheduler

977	    def compute_custom_metrics(metrics, key_prefix: str = ''):
978	        logs = {}
979	        # Synchronize keys to avoid getting stuck.
980	        if dist.is_initialized():
981	            all_keys = [None] * dist.get_world_size()
982	            dist.all_gather_object(all_keys, list(metrics.keys()))
983	            for key in set().union(*all_keys):
984	                if key not in metrics:
985	                    metrics[key]
986	
987	        for k, metric in sorted(metrics.items()):
988	            k = f'{key_prefix}{k}'
989	            value = metric.compute()
990	            metric.reset()
991	            if isinstance(value, dict):
992	                if len(value) == 1:
993	                    val = list(value.values())[0]
994	                    logs[k] = val
995	                else:
996	                    for k_suffix, val in value.items():
997	                        new_k = f'{k}_{k_suffix}'
998	                        logs[new_k] = val
999	            else:
1000	                logs[k] = value
1001	        for k in list(logs.keys()):
1002	            if logs[k] is None:
1003	                logs.pop(k)
1004	        return logs

1007	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1008	        metrics = self.custom_metrics[mode]
1009	        prefix = 'eval_' if mode == 'eval' else ''
1010	        logs.update(self.compute_custom_metrics(metrics, prefix))
1011	        return super().log(logs, *args, **kwargs)

# Copyright (c) ModelScope Contributors. All rights reserved.
import numpy as np
import os
import torch
from transformers import EvalPrediction
from transformers.utils import strtobool
from typing import Dict

from swift.loss.embedding import _parse_multi_negative_sentences, _parse_pair_sentence
from .base import EvalMetrics
from .utils import Metric


class EmbedddingMetricMixin(Metric):

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.add_state('last_hidden_state', default_factory=list)
        self.add_state('labels', default_factory=list)

    def update(self, last_hidden_state, labels):
        self.last_hidden_state.append(last_hidden_state.cpu().numpy())
        self.labels.append(labels.cpu().numpy())

    def compute(self):
        predictions = np.concatenate(self.last_hidden_state)
        labels = np.concatenate(self.labels)
        return self._calculate_metrics(predictions, labels)


class PairedMetrics(EvalMetrics, EmbedddingMetricMixin):

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        EmbedddingMetricMixin.__init__(self)

    def compute_metrics(self, eval_prediction: EvalPrediction) -> Dict[str, float]:
        predictions = eval_prediction.predictions
        labels = eval_prediction.label_ids
        return self._calculate_metrics(predictions, labels)

    def _calculate_metrics(self, predictions, labels):
        from scipy.stats import pearsonr, spearmanr
        from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import (paired_cosine_distances, paired_euclidean_distances,
                                              paired_manhattan_distances)
        embeddings1, embeddings2 = _parse_pair_sentence(predictions)
        cosine_scores = 1 - (paired_cosine_distances(embeddings1, embeddings2))
        manhattan_distances = -paired_manhattan_distances(embeddings1, embeddings2)
        euclidean_distances = -paired_euclidean_distances(embeddings1, embeddings2)
        dot_products = [np.dot(emb1, emb2) for emb1, emb2 in zip(embeddings1, embeddings2)]

        eval_pearson_cosine, _ = pearsonr(labels, cosine_scores)
        eval_spearman_cosine, _ = spearmanr(labels, cosine_scores)

        eval_pearson_manhattan, _ = pearsonr(labels, manhattan_distances)
        eval_spearman_manhattan, _ = spearmanr(labels, manhattan_distances)

        eval_pearson_euclidean, _ = pearsonr(labels, euclidean_distances)
        eval_spearman_euclidean, _ = spearmanr(labels, euclidean_distances)

        eval_pearson_dot, _ = pearsonr(labels, dot_products)
        eval_spearman_dot, _ = spearmanr(labels, dot_products)

        return {
            'pearson_cosine': eval_pearson_cosine,
            'pearson_euclidean': eval_pearson_euclidean,
            'pearson_manhattan': eval_pearson_manhattan,
            'pearson_dot_product': eval_pearson_dot,
            'spearman_cosine': eval_spearman_cosine,
            'spearman_euclidean': eval_spearman_euclidean,
            'spearman_manhattan': eval_spearman_manhattan,
            'spearman_dot_product': eval_spearman_dot,
        }


class InfonceMetrics(EvalMetrics, EmbedddingMetricMixin):

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        EmbedddingMetricMixin.__init__(self)

    def compute_metrics(self, eval_prediction: EvalPrediction) -> Dict[str, float]:
        predictions = eval_prediction.predictions
        labels = eval_prediction.label_ids
        return self._calculate_metrics(predictions, labels)

    def _calculate_metrics(self, predictions, labels):
        hard_negatives = os.environ.get('INFONCE_HARD_NEGATIVES', None)
        use_batch = strtobool(os.environ.get('INFONCE_USE_BATCH', 'True'))
        if hard_negatives is not None:
            hard_negatives = int(hard_negatives)
        split_tensors = _parse_multi_negative_sentences(torch.tensor(predictions), torch.tensor(labels), hard_negatives)
        split_tensors = [t.numpy() for t in split_tensors]
        can_batched = hard_negatives is not None
        if hard_negatives is None and len(set([s.shape[0] for s in split_tensors])) == 1:
            can_batched = True
        all_similarity_matrix = []
        all_labels = []
        pos_neg_margins = []
        if not use_batch:
            if can_batched:
                sentences = np.stack(split_tensors, axis=0)
                similarity_matrix = np.matmul(sentences[:, 0:1], sentences[:, 1:].transpose((0, 2, 1))).squeeze(1)
                all_similarity_matrix.append(similarity_matrix)
                labels = np.zeros_like(similarity_matrix)
                labels[:, 0] = 1
                all_labels.append(labels)
            else:
                for tensor in split_tensors:
                    similarity_matrix = np.matmul(tensor[0], tensor[1:].T)
                    all_similarity_matrix.append(similarity_matrix)
                    labels = np.zeros_like(similarity_matrix)
                    labels[0] = 1
                    all_labels.append(labels)
                    max_neg_scores = np.max(similarity_matrix[labels == 0], axis=-1)
                    pos_neg_margins.append(np.mean(similarity_matrix[labels == 1] - max_neg_scores).item())
        else:
            if can_batched:
                sentences = np.stack(split_tensors, axis=0)
                similarity_matrix = np.matmul(sentences[:, 0], sentences[:, 1:].reshape(-1, sentences.shape[2]).T)
                all_similarity_matrix.append(similarity_matrix)
                labels = np.zeros_like(similarity_matrix)
                for row, col in enumerate(
                        range(0, sentences.shape[0] * (sentences.shape[1] - 1), sentences.shape[1] - 1)):
                    labels[row, col] = 1
                all_labels.append(labels)
            else:
                all_tensors = []
                for tensor in split_tensors:
                    all_tensors.append(tensor[1:])
                sentences = np.concatenate(all_tensors, axis=0)
                length = 0
                for idx, tensor in enumerate(split_tensors):
                    similarity_matrix = np.matmul(tensor[0], sentences.T)
                    all_similarity_matrix.append(similarity_matrix)
                    labels = np.zeros_like(similarity_matrix)
                    labels[length] = 1
                    all_labels.append(labels)
                    length += tensor.shape[0] - 1
                    max_neg_scores = np.max(similarity_matrix[labels == 0], axis=-1)
                    pos_neg_margins.append(np.mean(similarity_matrix[labels == 1] - max_neg_scores).item())

        similarity_matrix = np.concatenate(all_similarity_matrix, axis=0)
        labels = np.concatenate(all_labels, axis=0)
        if can_batched:
            pos_scores = similarity_matrix[labels == 1].reshape(similarity_matrix.shape[0], -1)
            neg_scores = similarity_matrix[labels == 0].reshape(similarity_matrix.shape[0], -1)
            max_neg_scores = np.max(neg_scores, axis=-1)
            pos_neg_margin = np.mean(pos_scores - max_neg_scores).item()
        else:
            pos_scores = similarity_matrix[labels == 1]
            neg_scores = similarity_matrix[labels == 0]
            pos_neg_margin = np.mean(pos_neg_margins)

        mean_neg = np.mean(neg_scores)
        mean_pos = np.mean(pos_scores)
        return {'margin': pos_neg_margin, 'mean_neg': mean_neg, 'mean_pos': mean_pos}

33	If you want to provide us with a PR, please refer to [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/tests/test_align/test_template/test_agent.py) to write your test cases.
34	
35	## Callbacks
36	
37	The mapping file for callbacks can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/callbacks/mapping.py). Callbacks can customize the behavior at key points in the trainer. After customization, you need to register them in the mapping and use `--callbacks` to specify the corresponding callback class during training. For example, you can customize:
38	
39	```python
40	class CustomCallback(TrainerCallback):
41	
42	    def on_train_begin(self, args: TrainingArguments, state: TrainerState, control: TrainerControl, **kwargs):

If you want to provide us with a PR, please refer to [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/tests/test_align/test_template/test_agent.py) to write your test cases.

## Callbacks

The mapping file for callbacks can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/callbacks/mapping.py). Callbacks can customize the behavior at key points in the trainer. After customization, you need to register them in the mapping and use `--callbacks` to specify the corresponding callback class during training. For example, you can customize:

```python
class CustomCallback(TrainerCallback):

    def on_train_begin(self, args: TrainingArguments, state: TrainerState, control: TrainerControl, **kwargs):
        # Doing something when the training begins.
        pass

    def on_save(self, args: TrainingArguments, state: TrainerState, control: TrainerControl, **kwargs):
        # Doing something when save checkpoint
        pass
```

All callback classes need to inherit from `TrainerCallback` in base.py and override its methods. The interface is consistent with transformers' `TrainerCallback`, please refer to transformers' [callback documentation](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/main_classes/callback).

## Loss

The mapping file for Loss can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/loss/mapping.py).
Swift supports custom loss (currently only supports sft/pretrain/reranker/embedding tasks). After registration, set `--loss_type <loss-name>` during training to use your custom loss method.

Custom Loss needs to inherit from `BaseLoss` and implement the `__call__` method, returning a scalar Tensor. You can refer to [CustomCrossEntropyLoss](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/0d7c9f5bc0e7e7d67d914ce6edeb9ce24f60746f/swift/loss/causal_lm.py#L5) for customization. For example:

```python
class CustomLoss(BaseLoss):

    def __call__(self, outputs, labels, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
        pass
```

## Loss Scale

The mapping file for loss scale can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/loss_scale/mapping.py). In pretrain and sft tasks, the loss of trainable tokens is averaged, meaning each token is treated equally. However, in some cases, certain tokens need extra attention and should be assigned higher weights, or some tokens should not be trained. loss_scale allows developers to freely define their own token weights. (Pretrain and SFT support using loss_scale to control whether tokens participate in training and their weight sizes, while in RLHF, it only supports controlling whether tokens participate in training)

You can customize loss scale by inheriting the LossScale base class and implementing the `get_loss_scale` method.

```python
class CustomLossScale(LossScale):

    def get_loss_scale(self, context: str, **kwargs) -> Tuple[List[str], List[float]]:
        ...
```

The `get_loss_scale` function returns a Tuple. The first return is a list of decomposed strings, and the second parameter is a list of loss_scales corresponding to the strings. The float value represents the weight. For example, the following weight setting:

```text
["学习", "好", "数学", "是", "重要", "的"]
[1.0, 0.5, 2.0, 0.5, 2.0, 0.1]
```
In the example, we place more emphasis on the words "数学" and "重要" because their loss_scale is 2.0.

Of course, we also need to pay attention to the core logic of the `__call__` method, namely the influence of the loss_scale base strategy (base_strategy) all/default/last_round on loss_scale. For details, refer to the introduction in the [Command-line Parameters Documentation](../Instruction/Command-line-parameters.md). Also, refer to the influence of the 'loss' field in the dataset on loss_scale in the [Custom Dataset Documentation](../Customization/Custom-dataset.md).

```python
if loss or loss is None and (self.base_strategy == 'all' or
                            (self.base_strategy == 'default' and is_assistant) or
                            (self.base_strategy == 'last_round' and is_assistant and is_last_round)):
    new_context, loss_scale = self.get_loss_scale(context, query=query)
else:
    new_context, loss_scale = [context], [0.]
```

In addition, you can also use [JSON configuration files](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/tree/main/swift/loss_scale/config) and inherit the built-in ConfigLossScale class to customize loss_scale. Currently, two configuration methods are supported: exact string matching and regular expression matching. You can refer to the content in [Agent Support Documentation](../Instruction/Agent-support.md#usage-of-loss_scale) for understanding.

- Exact string matching, for example, refer to `react.json`, `qwen.json`. The JSON needs to contain a mapping of `Dict[str, List[float]]`. The string represents a keyword, and the list needs to have two values. We will split the string into multiple segments based on the keyword. The first value in the list represents the weight of the keyword, and the second value represents the weight of the content after this keyword and before the next keyword.
- Regular expression matching, for example, refer to `ignore_empty_think.json`, `hermes.json`. The JSON needs to contain a mapping of `Dict[str, float]`. The string represents a regular expression pattern, and the float represents the weight of the matching string.

How to debug:

```python
from swift import get_processor, get_template

data = {"messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What is today's date?"},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": (
        "<think>\nI can get the current time by calling the `get_date` function.\n</think>\n"
        '<tool_call>\n{"name": "get_date", "arguments": {}}\n</tool_call>'
    )}
]}

template = get_template(get_processor('Qwen/Qwen3-8B'), loss_scale='hermes')
template.set_mode('train')
inputs = template.encode(data)

print(template.safe_decode(inputs['labels']))
print(inputs['loss_scale'])
```

## Metrics

The mapping file for metrics can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/metrics/mapping.py). This component is used in both ms-swift and Megatron-SWIFT.

- If used in ms-swift, you need to inherit the `EvalMetrics` base class from base.py and implement the `compute_metrics` function, returning a dictionary `Dict[str, float]`. You can refer to [NlgMetrics](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/0d7c9f5bc0e7e7d67d914ce6edeb9ce24f60746f/swift/metrics/nlg.py#L33) for customization.
- If used in Megatron-SWIFT, you need to inherit the `Metric` base class from utils.py and implement the `update` and `compute` methods. The compute method should return a dictionary `Dict[str, float]`.

You can customize metrics (currently only supports sft/pretrain/reranker/embedding tasks) and set `--eval_metric <metric-name>` during training to use your custom metrics.

## Optimizers

The mapping file for optimizers can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/optimizers/mapping.py). If you need to customize an optimizer, you need to inherit the `OptimizerCallback` base class and override the `create_optimizer` function. Use `--optimizer <optimizer-name>` during training to specify the custom optimizer.

- You can refer to [MultimodalOptimizerCallback](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/0d7c9f5bc0e7e7d67d914ce6edeb9ce24f60746f/swift/optimizers/multimodal.py#L43) for implementation. This class implements the functionality of vit_lr and aligner_lr, which uses different learning rates for vit, aligner, and LLM respectively.

## Tuner Plugin

The mapping file for Tuner plugins can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/tuner_plugin/mapping.py). If you need to customize a tuner, you need to inherit the `Tuner` base class and override the `prepare_model`, `save_pretrained`, `from_pretrained` functions.

- prepare_model: This function is called before training to process and prepare the original model, wrap it with the tuner, and set trainable parameters. For example: you can attach LoRA to certain layers and freeze certain layers.
- save_pretrained: This function is called during training to save the model.
- from_pretrained: This function is called during inference/resuming training to prepare the model and load weights.

You can refer to [LoRALLMTuner](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/0d7c9f5bc0e7e7d67d914ce6edeb9ce24f60746f/swift/tuner_plugin/lora_llm.py#L24) for implementation. This class implements the functionality of performing LoRA training on LLM and full parameter training on ViT.

## ORM

Examples can be found [here](https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/blob/main/swift/rewards/orm.py).

ORM is an Outcome Reward Model. ORM is generally implemented using regular expressions. ORM determines whether a response is correct. For example:
